chapter_title: Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
text: In this subchapter, “manufactured house” means “industrialized building” as defined by Chapter 1202 (Industrialized Housing and Buildings) , Occupations Code, “industrialized housing” as defined by Chapter 1202 (Industrialized Housing and Buildings) , Occupations Code, or “manufactured home” as defined by Chapter 1201 (Manufactured Housing) , Occupations Code. The term includes a temporary chassis system or returnable undercarriage used for the transportation of a manufactured house and a transportable section of a manufactured house that is transported on a chassis system or returnable undercarriage and that is constructed so that it cannot, without dismantling or destruction, be transported within the legal size limits for a motor vehicle.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 623. Permits for Oversize or Overweight Vehicles
##### § 623.091 — Definition

In this subchapter, “manufactured house” means “industrialized building” as defined by Chapter 1202 (Industrialized Housing and Buildings) , Occupations Code, “industrialized housing” as defined by Chapter 1202 (Industrialized Housing and Buildings) , Occupations Code, or “manufactured home” as defined by Chapter 1201 (Manufactured Housing) , Occupations Code. The term includes a temporary chassis system or returnable undercarriage used for the transportation of a manufactured house and a transportable section of a manufactured house that is transported on a chassis system or returnable undercarriage and that is constructed so that it cannot, without dismantling or destruction, be transported within the legal size limits for a motor vehicle.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 2003, 78th Leg., ch. 1276, Sec. 14A.836, eff. Sept. 1, 2003.*

subsections.text: In this subchapter, “manufactured house” means “industrialized building” as defined by Chapter 1202 (Industrialized Housing and Buildings) , Occupations Code, “industrialized housing” as defined by Chapter 1202 (Industrialized Housing and Buildings) , Occupations Code, or “manufactured home” as defined by Chapter 1201 (Manufactured Housing) , Occupations Code. The term includes a temporary chassis system or returnable undercarriage used for the transportation of a manufactured house and a transportable section of a manufactured house that is transported on a chassis system or returnable undercarriage and that is constructed so that it cannot, without dismantling or destruction, be transported within the legal size limits for a motor vehicle.
